Liquid Wire Inc.。宣布已成功完成一轮1000万美元的首轮融资，将其可伸展电子技术扩展到医用可穿戴设备市场。这笔融资由迪尔菲尔德管理公司(Deerfield Management Company)牵头。Liquid Wire于2016年在美国俄勒冈州波特兰成立，已成为新兴液态金属电路领域的全球领先者。该公司使用印刷在塑料和纺织品基板上的无毒可印刷液态金属的专利类别，生产保形和柔韧的电子电路。通过更紧密地匹配人体皮肤的材料特性，液线电路允许复杂的传感器和数据传输电子设备进行舒适的长期接触，以进行身体上的远程监控。Liquid Wire的使命是生产可以自然共存的电子产品，同时直接嵌入功能材料中，这也满足了运动服装、汽车和航空航天市场的安全穿戴性能。Liquid Wire计划扩大其位于波特兰的试点生产线，并进行关键招聘，以利用首轮融资的收益解决医疗贴片市场问题。我们很高兴有这样一个备受尊敬的组织投资于我们的未来，并感谢我们现有投资者和客户的持续支持。Liquid Wire创始人兼首席执行官马克·罗奈(Mark Ronay)表示：有了这笔资金，我们处于有利地位，可以继续为我们的全球客户提供新的尖端可伸缩电路，使电子产品无处不在，特别是在医用可穿戴设备市场的新机遇。Deerfield合伙人史蒂文·霍赫伯格补充说：“我们很高兴为Liquid Wire及其创新的使能技术提供资金，这些技术可以克服目前可穿戴设备的设计限制。”它的平台在扩展新的数字解决方案的形式和功能方面具有广阔的潜力，这些解决方案是推进远程临床监测和管理的关键。

note_en:
Liquid Wire Inc. announced that it has successfully closed a $10 million Series A financing round to expand its stretchable electronics technologies into the medical wearables market.  The financing was led by Deerfield Management Company.  Liquid Wire was founded in 2016 in Portland, OR, and has established itself as the global leader in the emerging field of liquid metal circuitry.  The company produces conformal and pliable electronic circuits using a patented class of non-toxic printable liquid metals printed on plastic and textile substrates.  By more closely matching the material properties of human skin, Liquid Wire circuitry allows comfortable, long-term contact of sophisticated sensors and data transmission electronics for on-body, remote monitoring.  Liquid Wire’s mission to produce electronics that can coexist naturally while embedded directly in functional materials also addresses safety wear performance sports apparel, automotive, and aerospace markets. Liquid Wire plans to expand its Portland-based pilot manufacturing line and make key hires to address the medical patch market with the proceeds of the Series A round.  "We are excited to have such a highly regarded organization invest in our future and appreciate the continued support of our existing investors and customers. With this funding, we are well-positioned to continue to provide our global customers with new leading-edge stretchable circuits that enable electronics everywhere, especially new opportunities in the medical wearables market," said Mark Ronay, founder and CEO of Liquid Wire.  “We are pleased to support the funding of Liquid Wire and its innovative enabling technology that can overcome the current design limitations of wearable devices," Deerfield Partner Steven Hochberg added. "Its platform has broad potential to expand the form and function of new digital solutions that are key to advancing remote clinical monitoring and management."

note:
一月份在黄金海岸，来自FreeBSD基金会的Deb Goodkin以一张故意挑衅的幻灯片开始了她的Linux.conf.au演讲：FreeBSD，那只是另一个Linux发行版，对吗？这是开玩笑说的，以强调这是一个多么普遍的误解。
实现这一点的一种方式是在线介绍FreeBSD指南，通常在博客上名称中带有sysadmin、菜谱或教程；您知道我说的是什么。他们总是建议更新基本系统和pkgng，然后立即安装bash、nano、htop、lsof、coreutils、proc等等。有些人甚至将这些设置为内置工具的别名，甚至将bash设置为根shell。从那时起，你几乎不用去碰FreeBSD的用户空间。
就像一个维护不善的奶酪器皿，这曾经是用来磨碎的。如果您要安装整个GNU工具链，为什么不使用Linux发行版、Debian/kFreeBSD或专门为这些工具构建的类似Nexenta的操作系统呢？您没有了解FreeBSD的特性，也没有利用其内核和基础之外的任何好处。这是浪费机会，而且可能会使未来的项目贡献变得更加困难，因为对系统如何工作的假设被误解了。
自那以后，我稍微改变了语气，但有一个警告。我也想借此机会--而不是赞助商--为你的BSD笔记本电脑和休息室展示杰伊·帕特尔(Jay Patel)的RedBubble商店。我已经在下一批贴纸上加了一些。
我们应该鼓励Linux用户尝试FreeBSD，如果把他们熟悉的工具提供给他们，这是值得的。我个人学东西最快的方式是跳入深渊，但我知道其他人想要一步一步来。
在这场争斗中同样迷失的是FreeBSD，即使有所有那些专注于Linux的工具，它仍然是一个令人信服和有用的操作系统。能够提供所有这些工具，并且有时比Linux在相同硬件上运行的速度更快，这是一个特性，而不是一个错误。考虑到编写可移植的、符合POSIX的代码和应用程序的动机对大多数人来说不再是优先事项，它甚至可能更好地集成到原本完全运行Linux的商店中(SADFACE)。
因此，与其说这些指南没有用处，甚至歪曲FreeBSD，我们还需要重新定义它们。与其介绍FreeBSD，不如说它们是针对Linux用户的FreeBSD。这不应该被认为是批评；后一种帖子会非常有用。这样也更容易介绍特定于BSD的工具和想法，无论是在您介绍的每个Linuxism之后内联，还是在您比较和对比的后续帖子中。
我们需要在这两个社区之间建立更多的桥梁和联系，任何能让来自Linux或任何其他操作系统的人都可以联系到FreeBSD的东西都是有用的。
note_en:
On the Gold Coast in January, Deb Goodkin from the FreeBSD Foundation began her Linux.conf.au talk with an intentionally-provocative slide:  FreeBSD, that’s just another Linux distro, right? It was said in jest to highlight what a common misconception it is.
 One way this manifests is through introductory FreeBSD guides online, usually on blogs with the words sysadmin, cookbook, or tutorial in their names; you know the ones I’m talking about. Invariably they advise updating the base system and pkgng, then immediately installing bash, nano, htop, lsof, coreutils, proc, and more. Some go as far as aliasing these over the built-in tools, and even setting bash as the root shell. From then on, you barely have to touch the FreeBSD userland.
 Like a poorly-maintained cheese utensil, this used to grate. If you’re installing an entire GNU toolchain, why not use a Linux distribution, or Debian/kFreeBSD, or a Nexenta-like OS that’s built specifically for those tools? You’re not learning about FreeBSD’s features, nor are you taking advantage of any of its benefits beyond the kernel and base. It’s wasted opportunity, and could render future project contributions more difficult because of misunderstood assumptions about how the system works.
  I’ve since changed my tune somewhat, with a caveat. I also want to take this opportunity— not a sponsor—to spruik Jay Patel’s  RedBubble store for your BSD laptop and loungeroom. I’ve already added some to next sticker batch.
  We should be encouraging Linux people to try FreeBSD, and if giving them their familiar tooling gets their foot in the door, it’s worth it. I personally learn things the quickest by jumping in the deep end, but I know others want to take things a step at a time.
 What also gets lost in the fray is FreeBSD, even with all those Linux-focused tools, is still a compelling and useful operating system. It’s a feature not a bug to be able to have all these tools available, and at times run them faster than Linux could on the same hardware. It may even integrate better into shops that otherwise entirely run Linux, given the motivation to write portable, POSIX-compliant code and applications is no longer a priority for most people (sadface).
 So rather than saying those guides aren’t useful or even misrepresent FreeBSD, we need to reframe them. Instead of  introductions to FreeBSD, say they’re  FreeBSD for Linux people. This shouldn’t be constued as criticism; the latter kinds of post would be  hugely useful. It’s also then easier to introduce BSD-specific tools and ideas, either inline after each Linuxism you introduce, or in a follow-up post where you compare and contrast.
 We need more bridge-building and outreach between the two communities, and anything to make FreeBSD relatable to people coming from Linux, or any other operating system, is useful.
title: 将FreeBSD视为Linux发行版的教程网站
title_en: Tutorial sites treating FreeBSD like a Linux distro

这家搜索巨头今天还宣布，计划在未来五到七年内在印度投资100多亿美元。该公司表示，今年将在印度培训100多万名教师，并提供一系列免费工具，如G教育套件、谷歌课堂和Youtube，以帮助将印度的教育体验数字化。和其他国家一样，印度今年早些时候关闭了学校，以防止新冠肺炎的传播。
谷歌印度公司高级营销总监萨普娜·查达(Sapna Chadha)周一在一次在线活动中表示：“我们必须承认，并不是每个人都能上网。”她说，该公司正在与合作伙伴合作，通过电视和其他媒体带来教育。
谷歌周一宣布这一消息之前，其全球竞争对手Facebook也做出了类似努力。本月早些时候，Facebook与CBSE合作，推出了一项关于数字安全和在线福祉的认证课程，并为中国的学生和教育工作者推出了增强现实。
这家Android制造商还宣布，去年在印度推出的教育应用Bolo，帮助学生培养阅读和理解技能，将在Read All品牌下以9种语言扩展到180个国家。
note_en:
The Search giant, which today also announced  plans to invest $10 billion in India in the next five to seven years, said it will train more than 1 million teachers in India this year and offer a range of free tools such as G Suite for Education, Google Classroom, and YouTube to help digitize the education experience in the nation, which like other countries, closed schools earlier this year to prevent the spread of Covid-19.
 “We must acknowledge that not everyone has access to internet,” said Sapna Chadha, Senior Marketing Director at Google India, at an online event Monday. She said the company is working with partners to bring education through TV and other mediums.
  Google’s Monday announcement follows a  similar effort from its global rival Facebook, which partnered with CBSE earlier this month to launch a certified curriculum on digital safety and online well-being, and augmented reality for students and educators in the country.
 The Android-maker also announced that Bolo, an  education app it launched in India last year that helps students develop reading and comprehension skills, is expanding to 180 countries in nine languages under Read Along brand.
title: 谷歌在印度推动教育
title_en: Google makes education push in India

note:
在广受欢迎的视频游戏“半条命2”及其续集中，玩家与联合帝国作战。这个帝国可能是虚构的，但它有许多太真实的方面。半条命2的宇宙是用耐心和大量的工作创造出来的，人们可以从设计游戏最重要的建筑之一的试玩次数中猜到这一点。其结果是一个非常深思熟虑，有时微妙的宇宙，通常是看不见的，因为玩家更关心的是解决特定的谜语或进行特定的战斗。
在这篇文章中，我将不分析联合帝国，以回答它如何统治，它如何重塑人类群体的身份，它如何构成总体化文化，以及它背后的存在者等问题。注：本文使用了所有半条命游戏和其他相关来源的数据(游戏文件分析，Combine Overwiki或Half-Life Wikia的文章，以及开发者的评论)。里面有剧透。
第一个“半条命”游戏在美国秘密科学基地黑台地开始。在那里，一组研究人员正在研究一种外星人起源的晶体。这位玩家被放在其中一位的位置上，他是现在的传奇人物戈登·弗里曼(Gordon Freeman)，他领导了一项关于外星人水晶的实验。不幸的是，实验导致晶体“共振”，打开了连接地球和其他维度的多维门户。这个门户吸引了外来生物，外来动物涌入这个地方，并开始攻击人类。最终，开放的门户网站还吸引了一个危险得多的实体的注意：联合集团，一个已经跨越多个维度的帝国。联合收割机通过门户发动了如此巨大的攻击，以至于整个星球在七个小时的战争后投降了。
“半条命2”大约在20年后上映。戈登·弗里曼(Gordon Freeman)从时空的空洞中获释，在东欧的某个地方，他在一列火车上醒来。火车驶向17号城市，这是一个重要的城市，和其他地方一样，它已经被剥夺了原来的名称，只用了一个数字来命名。当他到达那里时，玩家发现了一个与苏联没有什么不同的宇宙。这座城市被分割成几个小的公共空间，由警墙和检查站隔开。市民从一条街到另一条街或街区必须通过检查站。他们需要执照才能住在自己的家里。房屋已经变成了集体公寓，由于警察的突袭，前门经常不见了。隐私和舒适都是过去式了。从检查站、巡逻队到飞行相机，到处都是警察。巨大的屏幕显示了新的人类官方领袖华莱士·布林(戈登·弗里曼的前老板)的面孔，他被联合收割机任命为他们的发言人。
联合王国最显着的特点是它是如何粉碎和使其他一切变得贫瘠的。城市建筑逐渐被吸收到一个“智能屏障”装置中，实际上是一堵不断扩张的金属墙，它“吞噬”了人造建筑。在蒙面警察的注视下，公共空间是令人心碎的奴役场所，而私人空间则失去了个性和亲密感。另一种装置，抑制场，通过阻碍一种关键的胎儿蛋白的产生来阻止人类生殖。院子里陈列着废弃的游乐场。沦为奴隶工人和行政编号，公民也可能是活着的最后一代人。
虽然只要停留在许可的地方，市民也是相对安全的。在联合统治的城市之外，甚至在它们被遗弃的地方，外来动物已经殖民了每一个可能的地方。下水道里满是藤壶，废弃的地铁隧道里挤满了头蟹，而海滩和洞穴里经常栖息着巨大的昆虫蚂蚁。所有这些动物都是危险的，并且已经损害了地球原有的生物多样性。联合收割机使用设备来保护自己的领土不受它们的伤害。它还将它们用作武器，例如将头蟹放入向人类叛军发射的导弹中。对联合收割机没有兴趣的地方就会腐烂。另一方面，吸引共同兴趣的地方被无情地开采：海洋被巨型水泵排空，并将地球上的水输送到其他世界。
七小时战争过后，城堡拔地而起。当弗里曼走出17号城市火车站时，他首先面对的是一座极高的金属摩天大楼。这座摩天大楼太高了，它的顶部被云遮住了。它通过高压电缆与城市的其他地方相连，并由圆形智能栅栏保护。凭借其蓝灰色的金属，典型的联合建筑，令人眩晕的高度，以及不必要的复杂细节，城堡是外星人统治的明显象征。事实上，堡垒已经建造在地球之外，并通过联合门户进行传送：城市17的堡垒已经被抛出
联合帝国及其堡垒似乎恰恰体现了海德格尔对技术科学的恐惧。它完全是以开发世界为导向的，无论是人还是物质。联合收割机收集它能收集到的所有东西，并根据它自己的组织进行移动。地球已经沦为纯粹的储备，不小心耗尽或被限制--或被遗弃。一切都从属于妄自尊大的权力意志。与城堡工厂和自然的外部消耗类似的是一个庞大的官僚组织：城市、警察巡逻、公民、船只和许多其他东西都有序列号，这意味着一个已经被技术科学积累的权力所框定的世界，也被一个管理当局的形式主义所框定。联合收割机的这一方面使它看起来像是海德格尔的技术“存在模式”的化身-根据海德格尔的技术“存在模式”，一切都是等待开发的股票-同时，它体现了被尼采称为“冷酷的怪物”的官僚管理国家。一切都从属于联合的整体，如果人类接受成为系统的活跃部分，那么他们要么被粉碎，要么被从根本上改变。
公民生活在贫穷和屈辱之中，居住在被剥夺亲切感的狭小空间里。他们通常营养不良。一些人加入了抵抗组织，这是一个秘密组织的松散网络，以避免联合收割机的控制，但由于这样的举动受到严厉惩罚，它仍然很少见。对于任何个人来说，逃离悲惨生活的唯一法律希望是加入联合收割机的行列，这通常是通过成为一名民防官员来实现的。属于CP意味着在系统中处于更高的位置。CP官员享受体面的餐饮，携带武器，并享有对同胞的一些(很小的)权威。
联合收割机的队伍对人类志愿者开放，但通过他们提升的成本很高。CP官员戴着可怕的面具，通过语音过滤器说话。他们的制服覆盖了他们的整个身体，使他们看起来完全是由工程设计的。作为一名CP官员并不意味着有一份日常工作，而是接受了一个全新的身份，然后互动只是作为它的一个体现。除了秘密帮助抵抗组织的卧底特工巴尼·卡尔霍恩(Barney Calhoun)之外，CP官员之间没有什么不同。CP官员享有体面的食物和社会地位，但为了赢得他们，他们必须背叛他们的人类同胞。
CP军官可以通过加入联合守卫来不断攀登阶梯。民防负责城市的治安，而守望者是一支军队。可以在探险、守卫监狱或偏远的工业设施中遇到守卫士兵。看守部队似乎比CP军官享有更高的地位，过着不那么繁重的生活。然而，从警察到Overwatch的旅程不仅仅是收到一件新制服和枪支：候选人必须接受跨舒曼手术。重新发动引擎的军官失去了一些记忆，身体上有突出的机械装置，其中一个似乎是喉咙阑尾取代了嘴巴。人们可能会推测，守望者士兵失去了说话和进食的能力，但这是通过他们制服中内置的设备造成的。
在守卫士兵成为精英士兵的情况下，这种模式再次重复。要做到这一点，他们必须再次接受手术：更多的记忆被移除，他们穿着一件特殊的白色制服，手臂上有一个头骨，还有一只独特的红色发光的眼睛。
一个人在联合职业生涯阶梯上升得越高，他的人性就被剥夺得越多。最重要的一步不是第一步。CP军官不过是身穿制服的人类。另一方面，守卫士兵已经在身体上融入了他们的工作。
最后，联合重组最极端的例子是在另一类仆人身上找到的：潜行者。这些是被手术肢解的抵抗者。跟踪者没有手或脚，只有螺栓固定在金属隆起物上，也没有生殖器。他们的大部分消化系统已经被移除，还有他们的头发，他们的脸部分隐藏在一个嫁接的金属板后面。跟踪者似乎完全接受了联合收割机为他们指定的生活，包括在工厂生产线上工作，修理机器和合成装置，以及维护能源核心-大多数这些任务都是在监督员的警觉监督下完成的。虽然潜行者被锁在城堡里，但抵抗组织知道他们的存在：“他们不会把我变成一个潜行者，”他们中的一些人发誓。
七小时战争后，黑台地的行政长官华莱士·布林(Wallace Breen)以自称地球代表的身份与联合收割机谈判。因此，他被任命为新的全球领导者，是联合收割机的面孔。布林的演讲被指定为“乳房”，通过巨大的屏幕和
当戈登·弗里曼到达17号城市时，他可以听到布林在评论“一个忧心忡忡的公民的信”。这封信抱怨压抑领域和不可能生育。布林感谢所谓的作者的“关注”，并通过积极的框架使限制合理化来回答。布林说，本能是一种低级的东西：“赞助者”为我们压制了本能，这是好事。他们有权为人类做出决定，因为人类不可能单独完成“赞助者”为他们所做的事情。干涉人类事务是以凌驾于自决之上的更高认识的名义是正当的。布林补充说，“压抑领域”既是一种考验，也是一条成为更好人类的道路：一旦人们证明他们“不再需要它”，并自由地放弃他们的“本能”，“领域”就可能被废除。
很明显，在他“睿智的老”外表背后，布林更多的是作为一名新闻秘书，而不是一名领导人。他为联合收割机的决定辩护。他的实际力量似乎相当有限：在“半条命2”中，布林只有在打破抵抗力量和追捕弗里曼时才会直接下达命令。在剩下的时间里，布林没完没了地倾诉他的广播。他享有的权力本质上是道德规范的塑造。他的行为似乎与许多现实生活中的神职人员所做的类似：通过利用“白人特权”、“性别研究”和左翼制造的其他元政治迷因，他们追求隐藏的上级的目标。
虽然布林对压制领域的合理化对玩家来说可能看起来很可怕，但如果一个人生活在这个联合主导的世界里二十年，他对它的看法可能会有所不同。不出所料，这种合理化掩盖了一个谎言：CP和Overwatch中最好的元素得到了“非机械的生殖模拟”的奖励，这表明性欲并没有真正被抑制，而是可以随意重新激活。
归根结底，布林是个卑鄙小人。他的情况比大多数人都要好：住在城堡顶端的办公室里，布林是极少数没有接受手术就进入那里的人之一。他背叛了自己的人民，支持联合收割机所做的一切，从而获得了安慰和官方认可。布林拒绝使用世界上的“联合收割机”，而是称他的主人为“我们的恩人”。
然而，就像在现实世界中一样，做一个无赖就像是和撒旦做交易：很少有好结果。在“半条命2”的结尾，一个逃亡的布林请求他的一位主人为他打开另一个维度的门。主人接受了，条件是布林进入一个新的身体。理性主义者相当不情愿，但很快就接受了。即使是布林，官方公布的地球上最强大的人，也无法逃脱再造手术的惩罚。
由于黑台地的多维门户，地球受到了各种外源生命形式的入侵。它们中的大多数可以被描述为动物，而且确实有一些-比如蚂蚁-显然是以现实生活中的动物为模型的。只有Vortigaunt人，一种特殊的外星人，后来成为人类的盟友，以及顾问(稍后将详细介绍)，被赋予了与人类相似的智力。
许多外源动物被视为资源，通过联合收割机进行重组，以成为合成军事单位。无人机和武装直升机分别是运输船和战斗舰，它们是被移植了模块的活体，使它们能够飞行和向敌人射击。漫游者也是一种巨大的三条腿的昆虫，配备了盔甲和激光。头蟹虽然没有经过改造，但被用作生物武器，放置在导弹内，向敌人发射。
也有一些外源动物被认为是无用的，因此不受影响。它们常常是危险的。它们对自然动物造成了毁灭性的影响，就像我们熟悉的猫、狗、老鼠和兔子被欧洲探险家带到偏远的地方一样。
这里要学到的一个重要教训是，虽然这些动物是问题，但它们不是玩家必须面对的最终问题。它们只是通常意义上的动物。它们既没有自己的文化，也没有社会组织(蚂蚁启发的蚂蚁除外)，它们四处游荡寻找食物。遇到它们的范围从轻微的恼人到彻头彻尾的危险。但主要敌人是联合帝国。事实上，联合体自己以两种方式利用这些动物：它以它们所代表的威胁来证明自己的铁腕统治是正当的，并使用武器化的头蟹来对付它的敌人。
同样重要的是要注意到，如果一个人有足够的技能和/或联合规则减弱，动物可能会反对它。在“半条命2”中，玩家必须击败一只强硬的守卫蚂蚁。在他赢了之后，他提取蚂蚁的信息素。使用这些信息素，玩家可以命令蚂蚁攻击守卫士兵，而玩家仍然处于隐藏和(几乎)空闲状态。在“半条命2：第一集”中，随着联合收割机规则的削弱，一些守望部队被头蟹推翻，士兵们变成了“僵尸”。
在现实生活中，以犹太教堂和银行为首的空心帝国和全球主义左翼分子利用“少数族裔”颠覆欧洲白人社会。他们训练和资助所谓的恐怖分子，以证明自己的铁腕统治是正当的。另一方面，他们鼓励大量移民涌入白人世界，然后鼓励他们成为“活跃的少数族裔”，积极攻击原始民族和文化。那些靠制度养活的“少数族裔”不只是一种烦恼，我们应该把重点放在他们原来的制造商身上。当傀儡“少数民族”转而反对他们自己的创造者时，这变得特别有趣。如果你已经完成了“半条命2”，你一定已经注意到，一旦你可以在“守望者”上安装蚂蚁包，而不是独自面对它们，游戏就会变得容易得多。这在现实生活中也是如此。
对于肤浅的观察者来说，在“半条命2”中，敌人无处不在。然而，尽管在游戏的每一关都必须面对奇怪的生物和机器，但压迫的核心在于联合设施，特别是在堡垒。(城堡和“新反动派”所说的大教堂有一个明显的相似之处。)。
在这一点上，你可能想知道谁是联合收购机的幕后黑手。“半条命2”在比赛的前几分钟播放了一段短片，展示了帝国的官方面孔。在整个游戏过程中，展示了许多组合结构和设施，以及无数的敌人。但很难看到任何人或任何东西真正引领这场合并。外源动物生活在野外。虽然他们有共同的外星人血统，但他们决不会对联合公司的决定负责。Synth部队、民防军官和守望士兵是执法者，而不是领导者。布林本人只是一名发言人，这意味着他并不是真正的负责人。那么谁是负责人呢？
在“半条命2”的大部分时间里，答案仍然是个谜。我们只知道地球上的合力与其他世界或维度沟通。只有在游戏结束时，我们才会瞥见一张苍白、毫无特色的脸，还有一双发光的假眼。当布林逃离玩家时，可以看到他在屏幕上与其中一个人交谈。他恳求存有为他打开次元门户，并不情愿地接受被移植到一个新的身体中。在“半条命：第一集”中，很明显，随着合并规则的取消，这些生物不能继续隐藏，必须暴露自己。
这些奇怪的蛆状生物才是联合帝国的真正主人。在联合统治的二十年里，他们隐居在城堡里，通过布林博士统治着地球。顾问们-他们最终被称为-住在城堡里舒适的个人豆荚里，与装配线和其他工业设施完全分开。
作为一个高度智能的物种，顾问们似乎是联合技术和结构的大部分(如果不是全部)的幕后推手。它们被赋予了心灵感应的能力，这使得它们可以飞行，尽管它们的身体像幼虫一样，没有附肢。当你看到顾问们的样子和他们是如何生活的，就会很明显，城堡的建筑是为他们量身定做的，只是后来才适应了人形：他们的吊舱在水平和垂直路径上平稳移动。除了布林和一些合作者之外，顾问们是城堡里唯一没有残缺不全的生物，这就证明了顾问们的统治地位。
虽然戈登·弗里曼不可能知道他们，但他对正确敌人的关注迫使联合收割机的主人们离开了他们舒适的藏身之处。在第一集中，玩家与一名顾问见面，并看到他们在不同的时刻通过屏幕盯着他。在这一集的结尾，他看到他们中的一群人在大楼倒塌之前，坐在他们的豆荚里逃离了城堡。
顾问们的力量来自于他们的思想。他们会给人心理上的打击。
.
note_en:
In the popular video game  Half-Life 2 and its sequels, the players fight against the Combine Empire. This empire may be fictional, but it has many aspects that are all too real. The universe of  Half-Life 2 has been created with patience and a lot of work, as one can guess from the number of  tryouts for designing one of the game’s most important buildings. The result is a very well-thought, sometimes subtle universe that goes often unseen because players are more concerned with solving a particular riddle or fighting a particular battle.
 In this article, I will analyze the Combine Empire in order to answer the questions of how it rules, how it reshapes the identities of human populations, how it constitutes a totalizing culture, and who are the beings behind it. Note: this article uses data from all  Half-Life games and other relevant sources (analysis of game files, articles from  Combine Overwiki or  Half-Life Wikia, and developers’ comments). It contains spoilers.
  The first  Half-Life game starts in Black Mesa, an American secret scientific base. There, a team of researchers are working on a crystal of alien origin. The player is put in the shoes of one of them, the now-legendary Gordon Freeman, who leads an experiment on the alien crystal. Unfortunately, the experiment causes the crystal to “resonate,” opening a multidimensional portal connecting the Earth with other dimensions. The portal attracts exogenous creatures, alien animals that flood the place and start attacking humans. Eventually, the open portal also attracts the attention of a much more dangerous entity: the Combine, an empire already spanning several dimensions. The Combine launches such a tremendous attack through the portal that the whole planet surrenders after a  war of seven hours.
 Half-Life 2 starts about twenty years later. Released from a hole in space-time, Gordon Freeman wakes up in a train, somewhere in Eastern Europe. The train heads towards City 17, an important city that, like other places, has been stripped of its original name and is designated by a mere number. When he gets there, the player finds out a universe not unlike the Soviet Union. The city is fragmented into small public spaces, separated by police walls and checkpoints. Citizens must pass through a checkpoint for going from a street to another street or block. They require licenses to live in their own homes. Homes have been reduced to collective flats where front doors are often missing due to police raids. Privacy and comfort are things of the past. The police are everywhere, from checkpoints and patrols to flying cameras. Giant screens display the face of the new official leader of humanity, Wallace Breen (the former boss of Gordon Freeman), who was appointed by the Combine as their spokesman.
 The most striking characteristic of the Combine Empire is how it shatters and impoverishes everything else. City buildings are gradually absorbed into a “ Smart Barrier” device, actually a metallic wall in constant expansion that “eats” man-made structures. Public spaces are sites of soul-crushing servitude, under the eyes of masked policemen, while private spaces have lost both personality and intimacy. Another device, the Suppression Field, prevents humans from procreating by hindering the production of a crucial fetal protein. Courtyards exhibit abandoned playgrounds. Reduced to slave workers and administrative serial numbers, the citizens may also be the last human generation alive.
 Although as long as they stay in the permitted places, citizens are also relatively safe. Outside the Combine-ruled cities – or even inside their abandoned parts – exogenous animals have colonized every possible place. Sewers are full of  barnacles, deserted subway tunnels swarm with  headcrabs, while beaches and caves are often populated by giant insects, the  antlions. All those animals are dangerous and have harmed much of Earth’s original biodiversity. The Combine uses devices to protect its own territories from them. It also uses them as weapons, for example putting headcrabs into missiles fired at human rebels. Places that have no interest for the Combine are left to rot. Places that attract Combine interest, on the other hand, are mercilessly exploited: the oceans are emptied by  giant pumps, sending Earth water to other worlds.
 In the aftermath of the Seven-Hour War came the  Citadels. When Freeman comes out of the City 17 train station, the first thing he faces is an extremely high metallic skyscraper. The skyscraper is so high that its top is obscured by clouds. It is linked to the rest of the city by high-voltage cables and protected by a circular Smart Barrier. With its blue-gray metal, typical of the Combine constructions, vertiginous height, and unnecessarily complex details, a Citadel is an obvious symbol of alien domination. Indeed, Citadels have been constructed outside of the Earth and teleported through Combine portals: the Citadel of City 17 has been thrown on the top of the original city center. Citizens cannot access the building but see it every day towering over them.
 Later in  Half-Life 2, the player enters the Citadel. He discovers a place of unusual architecture, full of vertical empty spaces, assembly lines, and power sources without security protection. Some of the lines inside the building can transport humans in the same way that they transport inanimate objects. The whole building seems made of the same blue-grey metallic and glass materials and sometimes features complex asymmetries that convey a sense of strangeness. There are humans there too, but the Citadel architecture was clearly not designed for them. A few exceptions aside, the only humans allowed into the Citadel have been surgically transformed into tools of the Combine. The other living organisms Freeman sees are exogenous animals that have also been surgically altered.
 The Combine Empire and its Citadels seem to embody exactly what Heidegger feared about  technoscience. It is completely oriented towards exploiting the world, human and material alike. The Combine gathers everything it can and moves it around according to its own organization. The Earth has been reduced to a mere stock, carelessly depleted or confined – or abandoned. Everything is subordinated to an overweening will to power. Analogous to the Citadel factory lines and the external depletion of nature is a huge bureaucratic organization: cities, police patrols, citizens, ships and many other things have serial numbers, which means that a world already framed by the technoscientific accumulation of power is also framed by the formalism of a managerial administration. This aspect of the Combine makes it appear like an embodiment of the Heidegger’s technological “mode of existence” – according to which everything is a stock waiting for exploitation – at the same time than it embodies the bureaucratic, managerial State Nietzsche called a “cold monster.” Everything is subordinated to the Combine totality, and humans are either shattered or fundamentally altered if they accept to become an active part of the system.
  Citizens live in poverty and humiliation, dwelling in small spaces deprived of intimacy. They are usually malnourished. Some join the Resistance, a loose network organizing in secrecy for avoiding the Combine’s grip, but as such a move is harshly punished, it remains rare. The only legal hope for any individual to escape a life of misery is joining the ranks of the Combine, which is usually done by becoming a  Civil Protection officer. Belonging to the CP means being a bit higher in the system. CP officers receive decent meals, bear arms, and enjoy some (small) authority over their fellow citizens.
 The Combine ranks are open to human volunteers, but rising through them comes at a terrible cost. CP officers wear a ghastly mask and speak through a voice filter. Their uniform covers the totality of their bodies, giving them a completely Combine-engineered appearance. Being a CP officer does not mean having a day job but receiving a wholly new identity, then interacting only as an embodiment of it. There is no difference between one CP officer and another one – with the exception of Barney Calhoun, an undercover agent who secretly helps the Resistance. CP officers enjoy decent food and social status but must betray their fellow humans in order to earn them.
 A CP officer can keep climbing the ladder by joining the Combine Overwatch. Whereas Civil Protection polices the cities, the Overwatch is an army. Overwatch soldiers can be met in expeditions, guarding prisons or remote industrial facilities. It seems that Overwatch forces enjoy a higher status and lead a less burdensome life than CP officers. However, the journey from police officer to Overwatch includes more than receiving a new uniform and guns: the candidate must go through transhuman surgery. Reenginered officers lose some of their memories and have  mechanisms protruding from their bodies, including one that seem to be a throat appendix replacing the mouth. It may be speculated that Overwatch soldiers lose the ability to speak and eat but through the devices built into their uniforms.
 The pattern repeats once more in the case of Overwatch soldiers becoming  Elite soldiers. To do so, they must go through surgery again: more of their memories are removed and they sport a special white uniform, featuring a skull on the arm and a unique red, glowing eye.
 The higher a human rises on the Combine career ladder, the more his humanity is stripped away. The most important step is not the first. CP officers are merely humans in a uniform. Overwatch soldiers, on the other hand, have physically merged with their job.
 Finally, the most drastic example of Combine reengineering is to be found in another class of servants: the  Stalkers. These are captured resisters who are surgically mutilated. Stalkers have no hands or feet – merely bolted-on metallic augmentations – and no genitals. Most of their digestive system has been removed, as well as their hair, and their face is partly hidden behind a grafted metal plate. Stalkers seem completely resigned to the life the Combine has designated for them, which consists in working on factory lines, repairing machines and Synth units, and maintaining the energy Core – most of these tasks being performed under the vigilant eyes of the Overwatch. Though Stalkers are locked inside the Citadels, the Resistance is aware of their existence: “they won’t make a Stalker out of me,” some of them vow.
  After the Seven-Hour War, the administrator of Black Mesa, Wallace Breen, negotiated with the Combine as a self-proclaimed representative of Earth. As a result, he was appointed as the new global leader and is the Combine’s human face. Breen’s speeches, designated as “breencasts,” are broadcasted through giant screens and the citizens’ televisions.
 When Gordon Freeman arrives at City 17, he can hear Breen commenting on “the letter of a concerned citizen.” The letter complains about the Suppression Field and the impossibility of procreation. Breen thanks the purported author for his “concern” and answers by rationalizing the constraint through positive framing. Instinct, Breen says, is something low: it is good that the “benefactors” have suppressed it for us. They were entitled to decide for humanity, because humans could not have done alone what the “benefactors” made for them. The interference in human affairs is justified in the name of a higher realization that overrides self-determination. The Suppression Field, Breen adds, is a test as well as a path for becoming better humans: once people prove they “don’t need it anymore” and freely renounce their “instincts,” the Field may be abolished.
 It is clear that, behind his “wise old” appearance, Breen acts more as a press secretary than as a leader. He rationalizes the Combine’s decisions. His real power seems quite limited: in  Half-Life 2 Breen only gives direct orders when it comes to breaking the Resistance and chasing Freeman. The rest of the time, Breen endlessly pours out his broadcasts. The power he enjoys is essentially moral norm-shaping. His behavior seems analogous to what many real-life clerics do: by working with “white privilege,” “gender studies,” and other metapolitical memes forged by the Left, they pursue the goals of their  hidden superiors.
 Though Breen’s rationalization of the Suppression Field may seem appalling to the player, one’s perception of it might be different if one had lived twenty years in the Combine-dominated world. Unsurprisingly, the rationalization covers a lie: the best elements of the CP and Overwatch are rewarded with “non-mechanical reproductive simulation,” indicating that the sexual desire is not really suppressed but reactivatable at will.
 Ultimately, Breen is a  shab. His situation is comparatively better than most individuals’: living in an office at the top of the Citadel, Breen is one of the very few humans to have been admitted there without undergoing surgery. He has achieved comfort and official recognition by betraying his own people and endorsing everything the Combine perpetrated. Breen refuses to employ the world “Combine,” calling his masters “our benefactors” instead.
 Just like in the real world, though, being a shab is like making a deal with Satan: it rarely ends well. At the end of  Half-Life 2, a fleeing Breen asks one of his masters to open him the door of another dimension. The master accepts on the condition that Breen enters a new body. The rationalizer is quite reluctant but quickly accepts. Not even Breen, officially the most powerful human on the planet, can escape reengineering surgery.
  As a result of the multidimensional portal in Black Mesa, the Earth has been invaded by a variety of exogenous life forms. Most of them can be described as animals, and indeed some – like the Antlions – were clearly modeled on real-life animals. Only the Vortigaunts, a specific breed of aliens that became an ally of humankind, and the Advisors (more on them later), are endowed with a human-like intelligence.
 Many exogenous animals are treated as resources and reengineered by the Combine in order to become Synth military units. The  Dropship and the  Gunship, respectively transport and fighting vessels, are living organisms that have been grafted with modules allowing them to fly and shoot at enemies. The  Strider, too, is a giant three-legged insect equipped with armor and a laser. Headcrabs, though not reengineered, are used as a biological weapon, placed inside missiles and fired at enemies.
 There are also exogenous animals that the Combine consider useless and consequently leave alone. They are often dangerous. They had a devastating impact on the natural fauna, not unlike our familiar cats, dogs, rats, and rabbits when brought to remote places by European explorers.
 An important lesson to be learned here is that though those animals are problems, they are not the ultimate problem the player must face. They are just animals in the usual sense. Having neither culture of their own nor social organization (the ant-inspired Antlions aside), they wander in search for food. Encountering them ranges from mildly annoying to downright dangerous. But the main enemy is the Combine Empire. Indeed, the Combine themselves make use the animals in a twofold manner: it justifies its own iron rule by the threat they represent and uses weaponized headcrabs against its enemies.
 It is also important to notice that if one is skilled enough and/or the Combine rule weakens, the animals can be turned against it. In  Half-Life 2, the player must beat a tough Guardian Antlion. After he wins, he extracts the antlion’s pheromones. Using these pheromones, the player can order antlions to attack Overwatch soldiers while the player remains hidden and (almost) idle. In  Half-Life 2: Episode 1, as the rule of the Combine is weakening, some of the Overwatch forces are overthrown by headcrabs, and soldiers turn into “ zombines.”
 In real life, the Hollow Empire — led by the synagogue and the  bank — and the globalist Left use “minorities” to subvert White European societies. They  train and finance so-called terrorists in order to justify their own iron rule. On the other hand, they encourage massive influxes of immigrants in the White world, then encourage them to be “active minorities” aggressively attacking the original  ethnos and culture. Those System-fed “minorities” are more than an annoyance, but we should focus on their original manufacturers. It becomes especially interesting when the golem “minorities”  turn against their own creators. If you have finished  Half-Life 2, you must have noticed how the game gets much easier once you can sic packs of antlions on the Overwatch instead of facing them alone. This is true in real life too.
 For a superficial observer, the enemy is everywhere in  Half-Life 2. Yet, though peculiar beings and machines must be faced at each level of the game, the core of the oppression lies in Combine facilities and especially in the Citadel. (There is an obvious analogy between the Citadel and what the “neoreactionaries” call  the Cathedral.).
  At this point, you might wonder who is behind the Combine.  Half-Life 2 shows the official face of the Empire by featuring a breencast during the first minutes of the game. The whole course of the game shows many Combine structures and facilities, as well as numerous enemies. But it is hard to see anyone or anything actually  leading the Combine. The exogenous animals live in the wild. Though sharing an alien origin, they are by no means responsible for what the Combine decides. Synth units, Civil Protection officers, and the Overwatch soldiers are enforcers but not leaders. Breen himself is merely a spokesperson, which means he is not really in charge. So who is in charge?
 The answer remains a mystery during much of  Half-Life 2. We merely know that the Combine forces on Earth communicate with other worlds or dimensions. Only at the end of the game do we glimpse a pale, featureless face with glowing artificial eyes. When Breen flees the player, he can be seen talking with one of these beings on a screen. He begs the being to open the dimensional portal for him and reluctantly accepts being grafted into a new body. In  Half-Life: Episode 1, it becomes clear that as the Combine rule is pulled down, these beings cannot remain hidden and must expose themselves.
 These strange maggot-like beings are the actual masters of the Combine Empire. During the twenty years of Combine rule, they lived secluded in the Citadel, ruling the Earth through Dr. Breen. The Advisors – as they are eventually called – live in comfortable individual pods in the Citadel, completely separated from the assembly lines and other industrial facilities.
 A highly intelligent species, the Advisors seem to be behind most, if not all, of the Combine technology and structures. They are endowed with telekinetic powers which allow them to fly, though their bodies are grub-like and have no appendages. When one sees what the Advisors look like and how they live, it becomes apparent that the Citadel architecture was made for them and only later adapted to humanoids: their pods move smoothly through horizontal and vertical paths. A proof of the Advisors’ domination is the fact that – Breen and some collaborators aside – they are the only non-mutilated beings inside the Citadel.
 Though Gordon Freeman could not know about them, his focus on the right enemy forces the Combine masters to exit their comfy hideouts. During  Episode 1, the player physically meets an Advisor and sees them staring at him through screens at various moments. At the end of the episode he sees a bunch of them fleeing the Citadel in their pods before the building collapses.
 The power of the Advisors comes from their minds. They can give psychological blows
......
title: “半条命”联合帝国

note:
前段时间我在一家银行工作时，有一个了不起的遗留系统。
这可以概括为内部的AWS lambda等效项。该产品包括完整的CI、CD、单元测试、代码审查、自动部署等…。
您可以编写一个python脚本(python是唯一受支持的语言)，并在大约5分钟内对其进行审查并将其推送到生产环境中。准备好供全世界使用。
这个东西最初是在2008年创造的，花了几个星期的时间。核心从一开始就基本保持不变，但在外围有了一些发展(代码审查、日志记录、性能、身份验证)。
从官方来看，这不是一个“战略”解决方案，它不存在，也没有宣传。
在非正式的情况下，它被多个行业使用，而且不断有新的发展。它太棒了，让人无法放弃。
我记得我们部门的暑期实习生们利用这个东西(暑期应该是2-3个月)，在3-4周后几乎完成了他们的小项目。而其他实习生群体还没有想好如何将应用程序放在当期的战略框架中。
最后一次统计有500多个应用程序在那里运行。更值得注意的是，生产日志显示其中80%处于活跃使用状态。
可悲的是，在不断搅动的软件世界里，每一件好事都必须结束。这件事最终将不得不重新制作或丢弃：Python2.7即将报废。已放弃验证库。中间件已经很长时间没有重新编译或升级了。也有一些潜在的UI改进亟待完成。
长话短说，它是由我升级的，我变成了软件考古学家。升级到同时支持Python2.7和Python3.7，支持OpenID Connect和Kerberos，在这里和那里添加一些消息和错误以简化使用，更新开发人员文档等…。
在升级到第一批生产脚本时，发生了一件更值得注意的事情。
两个用户每隔一小时报告问题。已调查此问题，并仅限于Internet Explorer。有些东西在internet explorer中不能很好地工作。嗯。
事实证明，该银行在一段时间前就弃用了IE浏览器，转而使用谷歌Chrome浏览器(#Strategic)。全公司范围内的员工(250K)积极远离使用Internet Explorer。试图在IE中打开一些网站被屏蔽，显示一个完整的错误页面，告诉你改用Chrome。这对用户导航有副作用，因为所有东西都是互连的(想象一下report.example.com链接到therreport.example.com并调用内部化api.example.com，其中一些URL被阻止)。
好消息是，由于最近的升级，这不是一次倒退，这很容易解决。屏蔽了Internet Explorer，并向5000名开发人员发送了不再支持Internet Explorer的通知。问题解决了！
[参考：为了用户体验，最好硬屏蔽IE，IE用户习惯于在其他浏览器中重试。也许过去几年开发的内部应用中有三分之一在Chrome之外根本无法运行，页面仍然是空白的，或者到处都是坏掉的小工具。]。
该脚本最初是在十多年前创建的，也就是框架创建后的几个月。这是最早发布的应用程序之一，从历史上看可能是第8个(现在有500多个)。
它实际上不是代码，而是某种模板。所有人都不知道。该框架提供了自己的模板语言和例程(21世纪的开发人员注意：请使用JJJA进行模板)。除了最后一件事之外，使用模板功能已经没有留下任何东西了，它以某种方式逃避了日志记录。做了一些调整，一切都恢复正常了。
经过12年的可靠和忠诚的服务，这一传统服务并未受到抨击。恰恰相反。它正在充分发挥作用，而且肯定会再活12年，比现有的所有javascript和战略框架都要持久。
唯一的缺点是lambda请求的启动时间过长(5秒基线)。它可以提供偶尔访问的报告和API。它不适合高性能的API或缓存，其他技术会更好地体现出来，强烈建议将Tornado作为Web服务器和Web框架。
note_en:
There was one remarkable legacy system when I was working in a  bank sometime back.
 This could be summarized as an internal AWS lambda equivalent. The offering was complete with full CI, CD, unit tests, code review, automatic deployment, etc…
 You could write a python script (python is the only supported language) and get it reviewed and pushed to production in about 5 minutes. Ready for the world to use.
 The thing was originally created in 2008 over a few weeks. The core remained mostly still since inception but there were a number of evolution at the periphery (code review, logging, performance, authentication).
 Officially it wasn’t a “strategic” solution, it didn’t exist and wasn’t advertised.
 Unofficially it was quite used, by multiple lines of business, and there continued to be new developments from time to time. It was just too great to abandon.
 I recall summer interns in our department who’s got their small project almost completed after 3-4 weeks leveraging this thing (summer is supposed to be 2-3 months). While other groups of interns had not figured out how to ship an application in the strategic framework du jour.
 The last count had more than 500 applications running there. More remarkably, production logs shown that 80% of these were in active use.
   Sadly, every good thing must come to an end in the ever churning wold of software. The thing would eventually have to be reworked or thrown away: Python 2.7 was going end of life. Authentication libraries were abandoned. Middleware had not been recompiled or upgraded in a long long time. There were a few latent UI improvements begging to be done too.
 Long story short, it was upgraded, by me, turned software archeologist. Upgraded to support both python 2.7 and python 3.7, to support OpenID Connect and Kerberos, to add a few messages and errors here and there to ease usage, update the developer documentation, etc…
 There was one more remarkable thing that happened while staging the upgrade to the first batch of production scripts.
 Two users reported issues at one hour interval. Investigated the issue and it was limited to internet explorer. Some things didn’t work perfectly in internet explorer. Meh.
 Turns out, the bank had deprecated Internet Explorer some time ago, in favor of Google Chrome (#strategic). Employees firmwide (250k) were actively pushed away from using Internet Explorer. Trying to open some sites in IE got blocked, displaying a full error page saying to use Chrome instead. This had side effect on user navigation because everything is interconnected (imagine  report.example.com linking to  otherreport.example.com and calling  internalapi.example.com with some of URLs blocked).
 Good news, this wasn’t a regression due to the recent upgrade and this was easy to sort out. Blocked Internet Explorer and sent a notice to the 5000 developers that Internet Explorer is no longer supported. Problem solved!
 [For reference: It’s best to hard block IE for user experience, IE users are used to retrying in another browser. Maybe one third of internal apps developed in the last years don’t work at all outside of Chrome, the page remains blank or has broken widgets all over the place.]
   The script was originally created more than a decade ago, in the months after the framework was created. This was one of the very first applications to be shipped, maybe the 8th judging from the history (there are more than 500 now).
 It was not actually code, but a template of sort.  Unbeknownst to all. The framework had came with its own templating language and routines ( note to developers in the XXI century: please use jinja for templating). There was nothing left using that templating capability, save for this ONE last thing, that had evaded logging somehow. Made some adjustments and there it goes, back in working order.
  After 12 years of reliable and loyal service, the legacy offering was NOT deprecated. Quite the contrary. It is fully working and it will certainly go on to live another 12 years, outlasting every javascript and strategic framework in existence.
 The only downside is the high startup time for lambda requests (5 seconds baseline). It’s fine for serving reports and API accessed occasionally. It’s not suitable for high performance API or caching, where other technology would be better indicated, highly recommend tornado for that as a web server and web framework.
title: 我见过的最了不起的遗产系统
title_en: The Most Remarkable Legacy System I Have Seen

谷歌首席执行官桑达尔·皮查伊(Sundar Pichai)今天公布了谷歌印度数字化基金(Google for India数字化Fund)，谷歌将通过该基金在印度进行投资。
我们将通过股权投资、合作伙伴关系以及运营、基础设施和生态系统投资来实现这一目标。这反映出我们对印度及其数字经济的未来充满信心，“他在该公司聚焦印度的年度活动上表示。
首先，使每个印度人都能负担得起自己语言的访问和信息，无论是印地语、泰米尔语、旁遮普语还是任何其他语言。
第二，打造与印度独特需求密切相关的新产品和新服务。
第四，在卫生、教育和农业等领域利用技术和人工智能造福社会。
印度是谷歌的一个关键海外市场，谷歌在那里的一系列产品和服务，包括搜索、YouTube和Android，已经打入了大部分网民。
印度是世界上人口第二多的国家，拥有13亿人口，今天有超过5亿人上网，超过4.5亿部智能手机在该国活跃使用。
“还有更多的工作要做，以使互联网对10亿印度人来说是负担得起的和有用的(…)。从改进印度所有语言的语音输入和计算，到激励和支持新一代企业家，“Pichai说。
尽管谷歌和所有其他美国科技巨头一样，从世界上最大的互联网市场获得的收入只占其收入的一小部分。但对于目前正在发展中市场寻找下一个数亿用户的任何在印度的美国科技巨头来说，这似乎都不是优先事项。
note_en:
Sundar Pichai,   the chief executive of Google, today unveiled Google for India Digitization Fund through which it will be making the investments in the country.
 “We’ll do this through a mix of equity investments, partnerships, and operational, infrastructure and ecosystem investments. This is a reflection of our confidence in the future of India and its digital economy,” he said at the company’s annual event focused on India.
   First, enabling affordable access and information for every Indian in their own language, whether it’s Hindi, Tamil, Punjabi or any other
 Second, building new products and services that are deeply relevant to India’s unique needs
  Fourth, leveraging technology and AI for social good, in areas like health, education, and agriculture
 India is a key overseas market for Google, where a range of its products and services including Search, YouTube, and Android have made inroads with much of the entire online population.
 More than 500 million people in India, the world’s second most populous nation with 1.3 billion people, are online today and more than 450 million smartphones are in active use in the country.
 “There’s still more work to do in order to make the internet affordable and useful for a billion Indians…from improving voice input and computing for all of India’s languages, to inspiring and supporting a whole new generation of entrepreneurs,” said Pichai.
 Though Google, like every other American tech giant, makes only a fraction of its revenue from the world’s largest internet market. But that does not appear to be a priority for any American tech giant in India that are currently searching for their next hundreds of millions of users in developing markets.

该倡议于2016年启动。淡马锡(Temasek)和摩根大通(J.P.Morgan)开发的一个原型去年开始进行测试，看看它与商业区块链应用程序的整合情况如何。
今天发布的一份由MAS和淡马锡委托的报告称，乌宾项目的原型通过与40多家金融和非金融公司的研讨会进行了验证。它的潜在用途包括更快、成本更低的跨境交易；外币兑换；以及托管和贸易的智能合约。
报告还表示，乌宾项目的原型可能会为与央行和其他金融机构进行更多合作铺平道路，以建立更好的跨境支付网络。
淡马锡副首席执行官Chia Song Hwee在一份声明中表示：“这验证了淡马锡在探索和构建专注于数字身份、数字货币和金融资产标记化的区块链解决方案方面所做的努力。我们期待着支持乌宾项目和其他应用领域产生的商业化努力，以期推动更多地采用区块链技术。“
note_en:
The initiative was  launched in 2016. A prototype developed by Temasek and J.P. Morgan began undergoing testing last year to see how well it would integrate with commercial blockchain applications.
 A  report released today, commissioned by MAS and Temasek, said Project Ubin’s prototype was validated through workshops with more than 40 financial and non-financial firms. Its potential uses include faster, less costly cross-border transactions; foreign currency exchange; and smart contracts for escrow and trade.
  The report also said that Project Ubin’s prototype can potentially pave the way to enable more collaborations with central banks and other financial institution to build better cross-border payments networks.
 In a statement, Chia Song Hwee, Temasek’s deputy chief executive officer, said “This validates Temasek’s efforts in exploring and building blockchain solutions focusing on digital identity, digital currencies and financial asset tokenization. We look forward to supporting commercialization efforts emanating from Project Ubin and other application areas, with a view to drive greater adoption of blockchain technology.”

note:
广告拦截器在网络上的使用很广泛，但用户也越来越多地屏蔽cookie和其他跟踪脚本。
Safari、Firefox和Brave等浏览器都注重隐私。许多用户安装其他插件(如uBlock Origin)以更好地保护自己。
考虑到阻塞率，Google Analytics仍然有效吗？由于阻塞导致的数据不准确有多大？我做了一个实验来找出答案。
在我的样本中，从2020年6月开始，在近28,000名独立访问者中，有13%屏蔽了谷歌分析(Google Analytics)。这从生活方式网站上的6%，美食家网站上的9%，到科技网站(你所在的网站)上的26%不等。
21%的笔记本/台式机用户、57%的Firefox用户、53%的Linux用户和65%的DuckDuckGo用户屏蔽了Google Analytics。
尽管苹果的营销重点是数据隐私，但只有5%的Safari和6%的iOS用户屏蔽了Google Analytics。屏蔽Google Analytics的Windows用户(19%)多于MacOS用户(18%)。
Google Analytics是安装在超过一半的在线网站上的最受欢迎的网络分析工具。似是而非的分析是我正在开发的一个新的、隐私友好的网络统计工具。
似是而非的分析与谷歌分析的不同之处在于，它看起来很现代，重量轻，加载速度快，不收集任何个人数据，与广告技术和监控资本主义没有任何联系。
值得注意的是，来自两个分析提供商的数据完全相等的情况很少见。似是而非的分析和谷歌分析有不同的度量方法，对某些指标的定义也不同。
为了跟踪独立访问者，Google Analytics使用cookie。似是而非的分析是一个无Cookie的工具，它以不同的方式跟踪不同的独立访问者。
因此，即使根本没有拦截器，似是而非的分析和谷歌分析之间的数据也可能会出现一些差异。
一些受众比其他受众更注重隐私，更懂技术，所以我比较了三个不同的网站，以更好地了解其中的差异：
站点1：技术站点。超过60%的流量来自有机谷歌搜索。60%以上的流量来自非移动设备。6月份几乎有10,400名独立访问者。你可以在这里看到公开的统计数据。
站点2：生活方式站点。70%以上的流量来自有机社交媒体。70%以上的流量来自移动设备。6月份有近8000名独立访问者。
站点3：美食家站点。几乎80%的流量来自有机谷歌搜索。几乎80%的流量来自移动设备。6月份有近9500名独立访问者。
6月份，这些网站上没有发布新的内容。这一点很重要，特别是对科技网站而言。
新帖子被分享到龙虾、黑客新闻和Reddit等科技社区，这将极大地增加屏蔽谷歌分析的流量水平。
我的想法是有一个更主流的受众来发现谷歌上的内容。这比点击黑客新闻上的链接更能代表普通网络用户。
考虑到似是而非的分析和谷歌分析以不同的方式衡量独立访问者的事实，我还观察了总的页面浏览量：
看到如此多的Firefox用户屏蔽Google Analytics也就不足为奇了。
火狐在最近几年进行了自我改造，并投入了大量的发展，使网络成为一个更快、更友好的地方。
火狐仍然是三大主流浏览器中最小的一个，用户份额只有7%。
狩猎对我来说是一个很大的惊喜。由于苹果所有的营销都聚焦于隐私，而且他们甚至强调Google Analytics被屏蔽，我原本以为数字更接近Firefox。
移动设备无处不在，是访问网络的最流行方式。但就安装内容拦截器的能力而言，他们也有更多的限制。
这从数据中可以看出，只有8%的移动用户屏蔽了Google Analytics，而21%的桌面用户也这么做了。
屏蔽Google Analytics的Linux用户数量并不令人惊讶，因为大多数Linux发行版的默认浏览器是Firefox。
但Linux也是操作系统中最不受欢迎的，只占整个笔记本/台式机市场的8%。
更让我惊讶的是，Windows用户屏蔽Google Analytics比MacOS用户更多。MacOS在最近几年已经成为主流产品，但我仍然希望MacOS的受众更注重隐私或更精通技术。
1%的搜索用户使用DuckDuckGo查找我研究中的三个网站，但其中65%的用户屏蔽了Google Analytics。
谷歌完全占据主导地位，97%的搜索量在前三大搜索引擎之间。谷歌所有的钱都来自广告，所以9%的用户屏蔽谷歌分析对他们来说可能不是最好的消息。
你的网站是关于科技话题的，还是你的受众使用Linux、Firefox和DuckDuckGo更精通科技？Google Analytics在很大程度上低估了你的数据，离开Google Analytics是一个明智的决定。
您的受众是否主要使用移动设备，并在iOS上使用Safari？拦网速度仍然不错，但没有太大的戏剧性。如果你真的不需要知道准确的统计数据，也不介意侵犯隐私的做法，谷歌分析可能对你仍然有用。
除非苹果真的开始屏蔽Google Analytics，就像他们的广告听起来那样。
note_en:
There’s widespread use of adblockers on the web but users are increasingly blocking cookies and other tracking scripts too.
 Browsers such as Safari, Firefox and Brave are privacy-focused. Many users install additional plugins such as uBlock Origin to better protect themselves.
 Is Google Analytics still efficient considering the blocking rate? How big is the data inaccuracy due to the blocking? I made an experiment to find out.
  Out of the almost 28,000 unique visitors in my sample from June 2020, 13% blocked Google Analytics. This ranged from 6% on a lifestyle site, 9% on a foodie site to 26% on a tech site (the website you’re on).
 21% of laptop/desktop users, 57% of Firefox users, 53% of Linux users and 65% of DuckDuckGo users block Google Analytics.
 Despite Apple’s marketing being focused on data privacy, only 5% of Safari and 6% of iOS users block Google Analytics. More Windows users (19%) block Google Analytics than macOS users (18%).
    Google Analytics is the most popular web analytics tool installed on more than half of all websites online. Plausible Analytics is a new, privacy-friendly web stats tool that I’m working on.
 Plausible Analytics differs from Google Analytics as it is modern-looking, lightweight, fast loading, doesn’t collect any personal data and doesn’t have any connection with ad-tech and surveillance capitalism.
 It’s worth noting that it’s rare for the data from two analytics providers to have complete parity. Plausible Analytics and Google Analytics have different measurement methodologies and define certain metrics differently.
 To track unique visitors, Google Analytics uses cookies. Plausible Analytics is a cookie-free tool and  tracks unique visitors differently.
 So even if there were no blockers at all, some differences in data between Plausible Analytics and Google Analytics were likely to be seen.
  Some audiences are more privacy-conscious and more tech-savvy than others so I compared three different sites to get a better idea of the differences:
 Site 1: Tech site. More than 60% of traffic from organic Google search. More than 60% of traffic from non-mobile devices. Almost 10,400 unique visitors in June. You can see  the open stats here.
 Site 2: Lifestyle site. More than 70% of traffic coming from organic social media. More than 70% of traffic from mobile devices. Almost 8,000 unique visitors in June.
 Site 3: Foodie site. Almost 80% of traffic coming from organic Google search. Almost 80% of traffic coming from mobile devices. Almost 9,500 unique visitors in June.
 No new content was published on these sites during June. This was important, especially for the tech site.
 New posts get shared on tech communities such as Lobsters, Hacker News and Reddit which would dramatically increase the level of traffic that’s blocking Google Analytics.
 My idea was to have a more mainstream audience that’s discovering the content on Google. This is more representative of the average web user than someone clicking on a link on Hacker News.
   Considering the fact that Plausible Analytics and Google Analytics measure unique visitors in a different way, I also looked at the total page views:
   It is not surprising to see such a high number of Firefox users block Google Analytics.
 Firefox has in recent years reinvented itself and has put a lot of development into making the  web a faster and more user-friendly place.
 Firefox is still the smallest of the three mainstream browsers with only a 7% share of users.
 Safari was a big surprise to me. With all the marketing Apple does focused on privacy and with them even highlighting Google Analytics as being blocked, I was expecting numbers closer to Firefox.
   Mobile devices are ubiquitous and are the most popular way to access the web. But they’re also more restrictive in terms of the ability to install a content blocker.
 This shows in the numbers with only 8% of mobile users blocking Google Analytics while 21% of desktop users do the same.
   The number of Linux users blocking Google Analytics is not surprising as the default browser in the majority of  Linux distributions is Firefox.
 But Linux is also the least popular of the operating systems with only 8% of the total laptop/desktop market.
 More surprising to me was that Windows users block Google Analytics more than macOS users. macOS has become a mainstream product in recent years but I still expected macOS audience to be more privacy-focused or tech-savvy.
   1% of all search users used DuckDuckGo to find the three websites in my study but 65% of them block Google Analytics.
 Google is completely dominant with 97% of searches between the top three search engines. Google makes all their money with advertising so it may not be the best news for them that 9% of their users block Google Analytics too.
  Is your website about a tech topic or your audience more tech-savvy using Linux, Firefox and DuckDuckGo? Google Analytics is under-representing your stats by a large margin and  moving away from Google Analytics is a sound decision.
 Is your audience mostly on mobile devices and use Safari on iOS? There’s still a decent blocking rate but nothing too dramatic. If you don’t really need to know accurate stats and you don’t mind the privacy-invasive practices, Google Analytics may still be useful to you.
 That’s unless Apple actually does start blocking Google Analytics like their advertising makes it sound that they do.
title: 我的网站访问者中有13%屏蔽了Google Analytics
title_en: 13% of my website visitors block Google Analytics

note: 在本周的Fedora工程和指导委员会(FESCO)会议上，更多的功能被批准用于Fedora33版本，该版本将于今年秋天发布。最值得注意的是Fedora33对默认终端文本编辑器的更改，但也接受了其他更改。本周FESCO决定的亮点包括：-将Nano设置为默认文本编辑器的更改获得批准。Nano将成为Vi的新默认配置。-LLVM 11和Clang 11.0以及朋友们将为Fedora 33而来。不过，LLVM11.0应该会在9月份发货，这并不令人惊讶。这将确保该LLVM工具链是最新发布的版本，并提供最新的AMDGPU、LLVM和其他编译器创新。-GNU Binutils 2.35已被批准用于这些最新的核心实用程序。-GNU C库2.32已被批准为GNU工具链的另一个更新组件。-Python软件的自动RPM依赖处理将进行更新，以便在需要时处理对Python额外组件的需求生成。-GHC 8.8和Haskell Storage LTS 13获得批准。通过会议记录了解所有细节。
note_en: At this week's Fedora Engineering and Steering Committee (FESCo) meeting, more features were approved for the Fedora 33 release due out this fall.   Most notable is the change of the default terminal text editor with Fedora 33 but other changes were also accepted. Highlights of this week's FESCo decisions include:   - The change to  make nano the default text editor was approved. Nano will be the new default over Vi.    - LLVM 11 along with Clang 11.0 and friends will come for Fedora 33. No surprise though really with LLVM 11.0 should be shipping by September. This will ensure this LLVM toolchain is the latest version shipping and also providing the likes of the newest AMDGPU LLVM and other compiler innovations.   - GNU Binutils 2.35 has been approved for those newest core utilities.   - GNU C Library 2.32 has been approved as another updated component of the GNU toolchain.   - The automatic RPM dependency handling for Python software will be updated for handling the generation of requirements on Python extras when needed.   - GHC 8.8 and Haskell Storage LTS 13 were approved.   All the details via  the meeting notes.
title: Feddora批准将Nano设为默认终端文本编辑器
title_en: Fedora Approves of Making Nano the Default Terminal Text Editor

note:
白宫团队最近将世界上运行速度最快的计算机添加到其40多个非正式网络中。
世界上速度最快的超级计算机与白宫不断扩大的超级计算努力联手抗击这种新型冠状病毒。
日本的Fugaku在6月下旬的全球超级计算机500强排行榜上超过了美国的领先计算机，加入了新冠肺炎高性能计算联盟。
该财团由能源部、科技政策办公室和IBM于3月下旬联合发起，目前为65个以上积极的研究项目提供便利，并围绕着一个巨大的超级计算机驱动的搜索，寻找与新型冠状病毒传播有关的新发现，如何有效地治疗和缓解它，等等。数十名国内和国际互联网成员通过这一努力自愿提供免费计算时间，提供至少485petaflops的计算能力，并稳步增长，以更快地生成针对新冠肺炎的新解决方案。
IBM Research董事兼财团联席主席达里奥·吉尔(Dario Gil)上周对路透社(Nextgov)表示：“最初是一个简单的概念，现在已经发展到拥有40多家超级计算机提供商，横跨三大洲。”“面对像新冠肺炎这样的全球大流行，希望这是一生只有一次的事件，研究人员推动发现的速度是寻找治愈方法的关键因素，我们必须联合各方力量。”
Gil和其他成员和研究人员向Nextgov网站简要介绍了这项工作是如何展开的，他们是如何衡量成功的，以及该财团越来越多地支持的研究。
能源科学办公室主任克里斯·法尔上周告诉Nextgov电视台，自该财团成立以来，其资源一直被用于筛选数十亿个分子，以确定可以快速制造并测试针对新型冠状病毒的效力的有希望的化合物，产生大型数据集来研究患者反应的变化，对一种新设备进行气流模拟，该设备将允许医生使用一台呼吸机支持多名患者-等等。这些复杂的系统在几天内就能完成计算、模拟和结果，几位科学家指出，在传统的计算机上，这需要几个月的时间。
Fall说：“从三个月前的一次小谈话中，我们克服了无数的机构和组织界限，成立了这个财团。”他补充说，这一努力是“建立一个由新冠肺炎研究人员组成的国际团队，他们正在分享他们最好的想法、方法和结果，以了解这种病毒及其对人类的影响，这将(允许)世界最终征服或限制这种病毒。”
在最近的一次采访中，能源部负责科学的副部长保罗·达巴尔解释说，任何对利用先进计算能力感兴趣的研究人员都可以通过在线研究门户网站向财团提交相关研究提案，然后进行审查以供选择。一个执行委员会支持该小组的组织并帮助指导政策，而一个科学委员会的任务是评估提交给该财团的研究提案在选择后的潜在影响。一旦超级计算机器被选中，第三个委员会就会分配超级计算机器上的时间和周期。
达巴尔指出：“从组织的角度来看，这件事真正有趣的地方在于，它基本上是一个志愿者组织。”
能源证实，截至7月1日，该财团已收到超过148万份新冠肺炎研究提案，其中78项获得批准，68项通过相关的超级计算资源启动并运行。尽管研究人员正在免费利用这些资产，但这项工作并不是没有成本的。达巴尔说，该财团利用了该部门的一些用户设施和资源，这些设施和资源是由纳税人的钱建造和资助的。这一努力导致了机器的运营成本，如运行时间、电力和冷却，达巴尔说，与一开始的实际能力建设相比，这些成本相对较小。
“这绝对是要花钱的，”达巴尔说。“但归根结底，这在很大程度上是利用了美国人民的投资，利用了灵活性，并将其转移到了这个关键问题上。”
综合起来，超级计算资源正在加快对新冠肺炎的答案和解决方案的追逐，但更快的速度并不是衡量成功的唯一标准。IBM的GIL在早期表示，“该财团的成立，以及我们在加快提案专家审查和将批准的提案与超级计算系统快速匹配方面所取得的效率，以及快速登录到这些系统上，将不得不被视为我们的第一个重大商业成功。”
这些参与者还通过启动和运行的研究项目的数量来衡量成功，并强调27个项目已经制定了实验、临床或政策过渡计划。内部人士还认为，他们能够迅速将行业参与者聚集在一起，正如吉尔指出的那样，“其中许多人是竞争对手”，实验室、联邦机构、大学和几个国际合作伙伴共享他们的系统是“一项重大的成就”。
周四，NASA高级超级计算(NAS)部门负责人Piyush Mehrotra告诉路透社Nextgov，NASA是从一开始就应OSTP邀请参与该倡议的财团成员之一。
该部门位于硅谷的艾姆斯研究中心(Ames Research Center)，托管着美国航天局的超级计算机，梅赫罗特拉指出，这些计算机通常用于支持NASA的航空航天、地球科学、空间科学和太空探索任务的大规模模拟。但是，该机构的超级计算资产中的一部分也保留给超出该机构职权范围的国家优先事项。
梅赫罗特拉解释说：“为了了解新冠肺炎，并开发治疗方法和疫苗，需要在流行病学和分子建模等复杂领域进行广泛的研究-这些研究可以通过超级计算资源显著加速。”因此，我们正在向致力于新冠肺炎响应的研究人员提供美国国家航空航天局超级计算资源的全部储备部分，同时提供我们的专业知识和支持，以便在美国国家航空航天局的系统上移植和运行他们的应用程序。
亚马逊网络服务(Amazon Web Services)是加入该联盟首批成员中的另一家，并参加了3月份在白宫举行的最初的圆桌讨论，这个概念就是在那里出现的。该公司负责公共政策的副总裁香农·凯洛格在5月下旬对路透社(Nextgov)表示，在加入该公司后，该公司“看到了一个明显的机会，可以带来云…的好处。参加治疗和疫苗的竞赛。“。自那以后，该公司已经为该财团正在进行的十几个项目提供了云计算资源，根据凯洛格的说法，该公司还向“研究团队提供了实物积分，研究团队为他们提供了云计算资源。”然后，这家科技巨头的团队定期与研究人员沟通，帮助解决技术问题。
凯洛格说：“这一努力表明，来自政府、商界和学术界领导人的合作和承诺可以如何增强研究人员的能力，加快他们的工作步伐。”
除了IBM、NASA和AWS，该财团的其他早期成员包括谷歌云、微软、麻省理工学院、伦斯勒理工学院、国家科学基金会，以及阿贡、劳伦斯·利弗莫尔、洛斯阿拉莫斯、橡树岭和桑迪亚国家实验室。随着财团的进展，它也在不断扩大业务。今年4月，国家大气研究中心怀俄明州超级计算中心、芯片制造商AMD和图形处理单元制造商NVIDIA等公司加入。
据高性能计算高级顾问珍妮特·莫尔斯(Janet Morss)称，戴尔技术公司(Dell Technologies)也于4月份开始参与这一过程。这项参与花了大约一个月的时间才取得成果，该公司现在正在捐赠来自Zenith超级计算机和其他资源的周期。
note_en:
The White House's team recently added the world's fastest computer to its informal network of more than 40.
  The world’s fastest supercomputer teamed up with the White House’s expanding supercomputing effort to fight the novel coronavirus.
 Japan’s Fugaku—which  surpassed leading  U.S. machines on the Top 500  list of global supercomputers in late June— joined the  COVID-19 High Performance Computing Consortium.
 Jointly  launched by the Energy Department, Office of Science and Technology Policy and  IBM in late March, the consortium currently facilitates more than 65 active research  projects and envelops a vast supercomputer-powered search for new findings pertaining to the novel coronavirus’ spread, how to effectively treat and mitigate it, and more. Dozens of national and international  members are volunteering free compute time through the effort, providing at least 485 petaflops of  capacity and steadily growing, to more rapidly generate new solutions against  COVID-19.
 “What started as a simple concept has grown to span three continents with over 40 supercomputer providers,” Dario Gil, director of  IBM Research and consortium co-chair, told  Nextgov last week. “In the face of a global pandemic like  COVID-19, hopefully a once-in-a-lifetime event, the speed at which researchers can drive discovery is a critical factor in the search for a cure and it is essential that we combine forces.”
    Gil and other members and researchers briefed  Nextgov on how the work is unfolding, how they’re measuring success and the research the consortium is increasingly underpinning.
  Energy’s Office of Science Director Chris Fall told  Nextgov last week that since the consortium’s  founding, its resources have been used to sort through billions of molecules to identify promising compounds that can be manufactured quickly and tested for potency to target the novel coronavirus, produce large data sets to study variations in patient responses, perform airflow simulations on a new device that will allow doctors to use one ventilator to support multiple patients—and more. The complex systems are powering calculations, simulations and results in a matter of days that several scientists have noted would take a matter of months on traditional computers.
 “From a small conversation three months ago, we overcame a myriad of institutional and organizational boundaries to establish the consortium,” Fall said, adding that the effort is “building an international team of  COVID-19 researchers that are sharing their best ideas, methods and results to understand the virus and its effects on humans which will [allow] the world to ultimately conquer or confine the virus.”
 In a recent interview, Energy’s Undersecretary for Science Paul Dabbar  explained that any researcher interested in tapping into advanced computing capabilities can submit relevant research proposals to the consortium through an online  portal that’ll then be reviewed for selection. An executive committee supports the group’s organization and helps steer policies, while a science committee is tasked with evaluating research proposals submitted to the consortium for potential impact upon selection. And a third committee allocates the time and cycles on the supercomputing machines once they’re chosen.
 “What’s really interesting about this from an organizational point of view is that it’s basically a volunteer organization,” Dabbar noted.
 As of July 1, the consortium had received more than 148  COVID-19 research proposals with 78 approved and 68 up and running via the involved supercomputing resources, Energy confirmed. Though researchers are tapping into the assets free of charge, the work doesn’t come without cost. Dabbar said the consortium taps into some of the department’s user facilities and resources that were built and funded by taxpayer dollars. The effort induces operating costs such as runtime, electricity and cooling for the machines, which Dabbar said are “relatively minor compared to actually building the capacity to begin with.”
 “It does absolutely cost money,” Dabbar said. “But at the end of the day, a lot of this is taking advantage of what the American people invested in, and using the flexibility, and shifting it towards this problem.”
 The combined, supercomputing resources are speeding up the chase for answers and solutions against  COVID-19, but that faster pace isn’t the only metric for success.  IBM’s Gil said in the early days, “the establishment of the consortium and the efficiency we have achieved in expedited expert review of proposals and rapid matching of approved proposals to supercomputing systems, along with rapid on-boarding onto those systems would have to be considered our first major success.”
 Those involved also measure success by the number of up-and-running research projects, and highlighted that 27 projects already have experimental, clinical or policy transition plans in place. Insiders also consider the fact that they were able to quickly bring together industry players, as Gil noted “many of whom are competitors,” labs, federal agencies, universities and several international partners to share their systems to be “a major achievement.”
 NASA is one consortium member that’s been involved in the initiative from the very beginning when it was invited by  OSTP, Piyush Mehrotra, chief of  NASA’s Advanced Supercomputing, or  NAS Division told  Nextgov Thursday.
 The division, at Ames Research Center in Silicon Valley, hosts the space agency’s supercomputers, which Mehrotra noted are typically used for large-scale simulations supporting  NASA’s aerospace, earth science, space science and space exploration missions. But, a selection of the agency’s supercomputing assets are also reserved for national priorities that transcend beyond the agency’s scope.
 “In order to understand  COVID-19, and to develop treatments and vaccines, extensive research is required in complex areas such as epidemiology and molecular modeling—research that can be significantly accelerated by supercomputing resources,” Mehrotra explained. “We are therefore making the full reserve portion of  NASA supercomputing resources available to researchers working on the  COVID-19 response, along with providing our expertise and support to port and run their applications on  NASA systems.”
 Amazon Web Services is another that joined among the consortium’s first wave of members and participated in the initial roundtable discussion at the White House where the concept emerged in March. The company’s Vice President of Public Policy Shannon Kellogg told  Nextgov in late May that, in joining,  AWS “saw a clear opportunity to bring the benefits of cloud … to bear in the race for treatments and a vaccine.” The company has since provided cloud computing resources to more than a dozen of the consortium’s active projects, and according to Kellogg, provides “in-kind credits to the research teams, which provide them with cloud computing resources.” The tech-giant’s team then communicates regularly with the researchers to help address technical questions.
 “This effort has shown how collaboration and commitment from leaders across government, business, and academia can empower researchers and accelerate the pace of their work,” Kellogg said.
 Outside of  IBM,  NASA and  AWS, other early members of the consortium include Google Cloud, Microsoft, the Massachusetts Institute of Technology, Rensselaer Polytechnic Institute, the National Science Foundation, as well as Argonne, Lawrence Livermore, Los Alamos, Oak Ridge and Sandia National laboratories. And as the consortium progresses, it’s also  expanding along the way. In April, the National Center for Atmospheric Research’s Wyoming Supercomputing Center, chipmaker  AMD and graphics processing units-maker  NVIDIA joined, among others.
 Dell Technologies also began the process to participate in April, according to Janet Morss, senior consultant, high performance computing. It took about a month for the involvement to come into fruition and the company is now donating cycles from the Zenith supercomputer and other resources.

note:
2017年春天，一对潜水员摇着鳍-首先穿过墨西哥尤卡坦半岛下一个充满水的洞穴中的一个狭窄通道。他们已经在洞穴系统中游了近半英里，绕着天花板和地板上伸出的岩石尖顶蜿蜒前行，最终到达了跨度仅为28英寸的门槛。
其中一名潜水员、昆塔纳鲁奥含水层系统研究中心(CINDAQ)主任萨姆·米查姆(Sam Meacham)回忆道：“那是通往整个另一边的门户。”
在小通道后面的房间里，有一个古老的场景，以令人惊叹的细节保存下来：一个有11000年历史的红色赭石颜料矿场，配有工具和火坑。今天发表在“科学进展”上的一项新研究描述了这个矿场，它是为数不多的揭示古人类在哪里以及如何提取充满活力的色素的考古遗址之一，这些色素在世界各地被广泛使用，包括葬礼仪式、洞穴绘画，甚至防晒霜。
“我花了很多时间想象过去人们收集矿物颜料的不同方式，”研究作者、密苏里大学考古学家、赭石颜料专家布兰迪·麦克唐纳(Brandi MacDonald)说。“但是能够在如此有趣的保存状态下看到它，这让我有点震惊。”
这一发现也难得地让人们得以一窥美洲一些首批居民的生活，他们在古代玛雅国家崛起之前数千年就住在尤卡坦。这些最早的居民之一是一位名叫娜娅的女考古学家，她很可能在大约1.3万年前在新发现的矿井附近的另一个洞穴中坠落身亡。在昆塔纳鲁奥(Quintana Roo)下方错综复杂的洞穴系统中，至少还确认了另外九名古人的身份，他们的遗骸在大约8000年前海平面上升导致洞穴泛滥后保存了数千年。
但科学家们仍在争论人们在这个黑暗的地下世界深处做了什么。他们在埋葬死者吗？寻找淡水来源？
“他们去那该死的地方干嘛？”国家人类学与历史研究所(INAH)水下考古办公室主任罗伯托·朱科(Roberto Junco)说，INAH是墨西哥考古的监管机构。“现在我们有非常非常有力的证据表明，至少有一个原因……是为了开采赭石。”
矿藏的发现始于一次偶然的发现，当时CINDAQ的洞穴潜水员弗雷德·德沃斯(Fred Devos)带领的洞穴测量班的学生在萨吉塔里奥洞穴系统潜水时发现了一条以前没有人注意到的隧道。德沃斯和米查姆很快就回来探险了。经过长时间的游泳和挤过狭窄的通道，潜水员们对他们所看到的感到惊讶。
该遗址是人类活动的时间胶囊：洞穴在会议厅地板上坑坑洼洼，散布着破碎的洞穴-石笋或钟乳石-这些洞穴曾被用作临时锤子。曾经照亮洞穴的大火留下了烧焦的岩石和木炭，被称为凯恩斯的整齐的岩石堆标志着矿工的道路。
“弗雷德和我立刻就开始指着所有这些东西，”米查姆说。“这不是自然现象，除了人类之外，没有任何东西能做到这一点。”
德沃斯联系了麦克马斯特大学(McMaster University)的地球考古学家爱德华·莱因哈特(Eduard Reinhardt)讨论这一地点。虽然莱因哈特最初持怀疑态度，但第二年他前往墨西哥，潜水穿过这段带着文物的洞穴，这段洞穴后来被命名为拉米纳(La Mina)(“矿井”)。“这个网站非常棒，”莱因哈德说。
更重要的是，采矿并不局限于一个洞穴。
米查姆说，在过去的潜水过程中，“我们注意到了这些奇怪的、不合时宜的东西”，包括成堆的岩石和排列在洞穴地面上的洞穴。莱因哈特说，然而，随着许多人在尤卡坦的洞穴中潜水，人们总是怀疑这些古怪的东西是来自古代还是现代活动。
现在，有了这样一个古老赭石矿的原始例子，研究小组得以确认，在昆塔纳鲁奥(Quintana Roo)拉米纳(La Mina)以南约20英里(约合20公里)处的水下洞穴中，至少还有另外两个疑似地点也可能在进行采矿活动。根据放射性碳年代，这三个地雷在1万到12000年前就开始使用了。
“这不仅仅是一次性的事情，”莱因哈特说。“当时有一个正在进行的勘探、发现和提炼赭石的项目。毫无疑问，还会有更多的分店。“。
研究人员与其他科学家和潜水员合作，开始记录确定的地点内的古代采矿活动。在100次潜水中，该团队总共在水下600多个小时，收集了样本和捕捉到的视频，以及数万张照片，以构建拉米纳遗址的三维模型。这项分析描绘了一幅丰富多彩的图景，展示了一代又一代熟悉这一景观约2000年的人精心策划的地下探险活动。
根据新汉普郡大学(University Of New Hampshire)研究作者巴里·洛克(Barry Rock)的分析，在矿井周围发现的木炭来自高树脂木材，很可能是因为它能够明亮而持久地燃烧。莱因哈特指出，该遗址似乎还保留了古代矿工挖掘材料的思维过程：矿工们沿着沉积层前进，直到赭石逐渐消失。然后他们侧身去挖另一个坑。“他们了解……直到17世纪中期才真正编纂或正式制定的一些基本地质学原理，”他说。
麦克唐纳补充说，颜料本身的质量也非常高，杂质很少，颗粒非常细。这意味着它很容易将其充满活力的色调赋予它所触及的一切。“它的污渍就像疯了一样，”她说。
然而，人们究竟在用这些丰富的色素做什么呢？赭石是一种富含铁的物质，世界各地的人类已经使用了数十万年。大约10万年前，在南非，这种颜料被用来将充满活力的浆液混合到鲍鱼壳中。它们照亮了大约3万年前法国肖维洞穴墙上举起的手的轮廓。他们给一名埋葬在西班牙北部一个洞穴中的妇女穿上大约19000年前的衣服。
赭石的用途也很实用。它可以起到驱蚊剂或防晒霜的作用。它可能已经形成了工具制造中粘合剂的基础。一些非洲原住民和澳大利亚人今天仍然将这些充满活力的颜料用于仪式和实用目的。
然而，对于在尤卡坦洞穴中开采赭石的人们来说，最终目标仍然不清楚。“在这个时候，我们只是不知道，”麦克唐纳说。
一些科学家在矿井中看到了线索，这些线索可能暗示了这一发现的仪式或精神方面。加州大学默塞德分校(University of California，Merced)的霍利·莫耶斯(Holley Moyes)表示，这些矿场位于地下系统深处，远离光线。莫耶斯专门从事玛雅洞穴的仪式使用，并不是该项目的一部分。她说，在人类早期历史中发现的几乎每一个例子中，这个所谓的“黑暗地带”的使用都仅限于仪式目的。
莫耶斯说：“洞穴产生各种各样的善与恶，它们可能是最神圣的自然特征。”洞穴被视为通往冥界的入口和圣水的来源，对玛雅人来说尤其是精神上的地方，他们在尤卡坦矿被遗弃数千年后首次在尤卡坦建造了自己的城镇。赭石对玛雅人和其他中美洲文化(如阿兹特克人)来说也是神圣的，并在艺术和仪式中占有突出地位。“这与红色有关，”她说。
加州州立大学洛杉矶分校(California State University，Los Angeles)洞穴考古专家詹姆斯·布雷迪(James Brady)表示，理解古代行为的部分挑战源于现代精神和实践的分离。布雷迪不是研究小组的成员。他说，对于今天的许多人来说，“宗教是周日早上的一个小时”。但这很可能不是几千年前的情况。“它来自一个神圣的地方，这可能是非常有意义的，”他在谈到赭石时说，“而且有一次专门为了得到它而进入洞穴的旅行。”
不管提取背后的意图是什么，研究人员都对这一发现感到兴奋。该遗址令人震惊的保存为了解美洲早期居民的活动提供了一个前所未有的窗口，并有望帮助指导未来对洞穴使用的研究。
INAH的Junco说：“我们在墨西哥非常高兴能参与这个项目。”“这真的是比赛发生巨大变化的时刻之一。”
note_en:
In the spring of 2017, a pair of divers shimmied fin-first through a narrow passageway in a water-filled cave beneath Mexico’s Yucatán peninsula. They had already swum for nearly half a mile through the cave system, winding around spires of rock jutting from the ceiling and floor, when they finally arrived at the threshold that spanned a mere 28 inches across.
 “That was the portal into this whole other side,” recalls one of the divers,  Sam Meacham, director of the  Quintana Roo Aquifer System Research Center (CINDAQ).
 In the chamber that lay beyond the tiny passage was an ancient scene preserved in stunning detail: an 11,000-year-old mining site for red ocher pigments, complete with tools and fire pits. The mine, described in a  new study published today in  Science Advances, is one of the few archaeological sites to reveal where and how ancient humans extracted the vibrant pigments that have been put to a host of uses around the world, including mortuary rituals, cave painting, and even sunscreen.
 “I’ve spent a lot of time imagining the different ways that people in the past have gone about collecting mineral pigments,” says study author  Brandi MacDonald, an archaeologist at the University of Missouri and expert on ocher pigments. “But being able to see it like this in such an interesting state of preservation, it just kind of blew me away.”
 The discovery also gives a rare glimpse into the lives of some of the first residents of the Americas, who lived in Yucatán thousands of years before the rise of the ancient Maya state. One of these earliest inhabitants was  a girl archaeologists named Naia, who likely fell to her death in another cave near the newfound mine some 13,000 years ago. At least nine other ancient individuals have been identified in the tangled system of caves beneath Quintana Roo, their remains preserved for thousands of years after the caves flooded from rising seas roughly 8,000 years ago.
 But scientists still debate what people were doing deep inside this dark underworld. Were they burying their dead? Seeking sources of fresh water?
 “What the hell were they going down there for?” says  Roberto Junco, director of the Underwater Archaeology office of the National Institute of Anthropology and History (INAH), the regulating body for archaeology in Mexico. “Now we have really, really strong evidence that at least one of the reasons...was for the mining of ocher.”
  The discovery of the mine began with a happenstance find when students in a cave surveying class led by  Fred Devos, a cave diver with CINDAQ, spotted a previously unnoticed tunnel while diving in the Sagitario cave system. Devos and Meacham returned soon after to explore. After a lengthy swim and squeeze through the narrow passageway, the divers were astonished by what they saw.
 The site is a time capsule of human activity: Pits pockmark the chamber floor, and scattered across are broken speleothems—stalagmites or stalactites—that had been used as makeshift hammers. Burnt rock and charcoal remained from fires that once illuminated the cavern, and tidy piles of rock, known as cairns, marked the miner’s way.
 “Fred and I immediately just started pointing at all of this stuff,” Meacham says. “It’s not natural, and there’s nothing that could have done this other than humans.”
  Devos reached out to  Eduard Reinhardt, a geoarchaeologist at McMaster University to discuss the site. While Reinhardt was initially skeptical, he headed down to Mexico the following year to dive through the section of cave with artifacts, which was later named La Mina (“the mine.”) “The site is phenomenal,” Reinhard says.
 What’s more, the mining wasn’t just limited to the one cave.
 During past dives, Meacham says, “We’ve noticed these strange, out-of-place things,” including rocks stacked into piles and speleothems arranged on the cave floors. Yet with many people diving in Yucatán’s caves, there was always doubt as to whether these oddities came from ancient or modern activity, Reinhardt says.
 Now, with such a pristine example of an ancient ocher mine in hand, the team was able to confirm that at least two other suspected sites in submerged caves some 20 miles south of La Mina in Quintana Roo were also likely mining operations. The trio of mines were in use between 10,000 and 12,000 years ago, according to radiocarbon dates.
 “It’s not just a one-off sort of thing,” Reinhardt says. “There was an active program to prospect, find, and extract ocher. There’ll no doubt be more locations.”
  Teaming up with other scientists and divers, the research crew began documenting the ancient mining activity inside the identified sites. Across 100 dives totaling more than 600 hours underwater, the team collected samples and captured video, along with tens of thousands of photos to construct a three-dimensional model of the La Mina site. The analysis paints a colorful picture of well-planned expeditions underground by generations of people knowledgable of the landscape for some 2,000 years.
 The charcoal found around the mines comes from high-resin woods, and was likely selected for its ability to burn bright and long, according to analysis by study author  Barry Rock of the University of New Hampshire. The site also seems to preserve the ancient miners’ thought process on excavating materials, notes Reinhardt: The miners followed along the deposit beds until the ocher petered out. They then shifted sideways to dig another pit. “They understood...some basic geological principles that weren’t really codified or formalized until the mid 1600s,” he says.
 The pigments themselves were also very high quality, MacDonald adds, with few impurities and a very fine grain size. This means it readily imparts its vibrant hues to everything it touches. “It stains like crazy,” she says.
 Yet what exactly were the people doing with this abundance of pigment? Ocher is an iron-rich material that humans around the world have used for hundreds of thousands of years. The pigments were  used to mix a vibrant slurry in abalone shells in South Africa around 100,000 years ago. They illuminate  the outline of hands held up to cave walls in Chauvet, France some 30,000 years ago. They coat a  woman buried in a cave in northern Spain some 19,000 years ago.
 Ocher’s uses are also practical. It can act as a  mosquito repellent or a sunscreen. It may have formed the base for  adhesives in toolmaking. Some indigenous Africans and Australians still use these vibrant pigments today for both ritual and practical purposes.
 However, for the people mining ocher in the caves of the Yucatán, the end goal remains unclear. “At this time, we just don’t know,” MacDonald says.
  Some scientists see clues in the mines themselves that might hint at a ritual or spiritual side of the find. The mining sites are located deep in underground systems, far from the reaches of light, says  Holley Moyes of the University of California, Merced, who specializes in the ritual use of Maya caves and was not a part of the project. In almost every instance yet found through humankind’s early history, she says, uses of this so-called “dark zone” are limited to ritual purposes.
 “Caves produce all kinds of good and evil; they’re probably the most sacred natural feature,” Moyes says. Viewed as entrances to the underworld and sources of sacred water, caves are particularly spiritual places for the Maya, who first built their towns and cities in Yucatán thousands of years after the mine was abandoned. Ocher was also sacred to the Maya and other Mesoamerican cultures, such as the Aztecs, and featured prominently in art and rituals. “It’s something about that red color,” she says.
 Part of the challenge in understanding ancient actions stems from a modern separation of the spiritual and the practical, says  James Brady, an expert in cave archaeology at California State University, Los Angeles, who was not part of the study team. For many people today, “religion is one hour on Sunday morning,” he says. But that was likely not the case thousands of years ago. “It could be highly significant that this came from a sacred place,” he says of the ocher, “[And] that there was a journey into the cave especially to get it.”
 Regardless of the intent behind the extraction, researchers are thrilled about the find. The site’s astounding preservation provides an unprecedented window into the activities of the early residents of the Americas, and promises to help direct future research into the use of caves.
 “We’re super excited here in Mexico to be working on this project,” INAH’s Junco says. “This is truly one of those moments where there’s a big change in the game.”
title: 水下洞穴中有11000年历史的赭石矿
title_en: 11,000 year old ocher mine in underwater cave

note:
许多人抱怨记忆力很差。购物清单、朋友的生日、考试的统计数据--这些似乎都不会让人印象深刻。但是记忆并不像你想象的那样是板上钉钉的。有了正确的技巧，你很可能能够记住几乎所有的东西。
Nelson Dellis是四届美国记忆冠军和记忆大师。他的一些记忆功绩包括记住10,000个圆周率数字，超过9副洗牌的顺序，以及只听一次就能列出数百个名字的名单。
但戴利斯说，只要稍加奉献，任何人都可以提高记忆力。这里有五个步骤可以让你的大脑充满信息。
让我们从一个相当简单的记忆任务开始：世界七大奇迹。为了记住这些，Dellis建议从把每一件东西都变成容易记住的图像开始。有些将会更加明显。例如，对于中国的长城，你可能只想想象一堵墙。对于佩特拉来说，你可能会选择自己宠物的形象。
“使用像这样多汁的脑海图像是非常有效的。你想做的是创造巨大的、多感官的记忆，“伦敦大学学院的心理学家、”记忆幻觉：记忆、遗忘和虚假记忆的科学“一书的作者朱莉娅·肖解释道。你要瞄准你几乎能感觉到、闻到和看到的脑海中的图像，使它们尽可能真实。
这一切背后都有科学依据。肖说：“奇怪的、可能粗俗的或情绪化的图像都是粘性的。”“研究人员在观察大脑时发现，杏仁核--大脑中处理情绪的重要部分--鼓励大脑的其他部分储存记忆。”这就是为什么强烈的情绪使记忆更有可能持久。
下一步是把那些强烈的心理图像放在你真正熟悉的地方。在Dellis的例子中，他把七个奇迹中的每一个都放在一条穿过他家的路线上，从他的入口处的一堵墙开始，然后基督-代表救世主基督-懒洋洋地躺在他的沙发上。“越奇怪越好，”戴利斯说。在厨房里，你可能会想象一只骆驼在做饭。
肖说，这种将图像与地点联系起来的技术被称为记忆宫殿，对于记住某些元素的顺序特别有用。“记忆宫殿充分利用了你对真实地方的现有记忆。这是一个你非常熟悉的地方--通常是你的家，或者你非常熟悉的其他地方。“
如果它是一个只有7个项目的列表，那么空间可能相对较小。但在记忆10,000位圆周率时，戴利斯不得不将他的记忆宫殿扩大到整个他的家乡迈阿密。他把10,000个数字分成2,000个块，每个块5个数字，然后把它们全部放在10个不同的社区。
“神经成像研究表明，当人们使用记忆宫殿学习记忆时，大脑[枕顶区]的活动增加，”肖说。“这意味着这项技术有助于引入更多通常专用于其他感官的大脑部分--顶叶负责导航，枕叶负责观看图像。”
记住世界奇观的七张奇怪的图片应该不会太难，但是当你要记住10,000位圆周率时，你可能需要更多的动力。“我会对自己说这句咒语。我想记住这个，我想记住这个，“戴利斯说。“这是一句简单的口头禅，但它会让我集中注意力，专注于手头的任务，帮助我更好地记住它。”
例如，对于非常大的数字，如圆周率-或一长串牌，它也有助于打破局面。戴利斯把圆周率的每一个五位数块都变成了他可以很容易记住的图像。“单词很简单，你看到一个单词，它通常会在你的脑海中唤起某种意象。但是像数字、卡片甚至名字这样的事情就有点棘手了，“他说。“而且这些系统都是我们开发和学习的，所以每当我们看到一个名字、号码或卡片时，我们就已经为它预设了一个图像。”
对于Dellis来说，小数点后的前五位圆周率由身穿钢铁侠西装的山姆·尼尔代表(他说：“就是这样”)。第二块是他的朋友打扮成电影“角斗士”中皇帝的形象，拇指朝下。
一旦你整理好了你的图像，填满了你的记忆宫殿，你只需要真正确保它们会留在你的脑海里。“大多数记忆永远不会进入你的长期记忆，”肖说。“这就是为什么重复信息、将短期记忆转化为长期记忆是如此重要的原因。”
可悲的是，要做到这一点并不是一件容易的事。归根结底，这需要重复和努力工作。“为了成为记忆冠军，我必须每天努力。刻苦训练，让它成为我的本能。而这只有通过练习才能实现。“戴利斯说。如果这听起来工作量很大，那么，你就是对的。
